evals.prompt: I just installed Clams and I want to track my Bitcoin cold storage. I have an xpub from my Coldcard: `xpub6CUGRUonZSQ4TWtTMmzXdrXDtypWKiKrhko4egpiMZbpiaQL2jkwSB1icqYh2cfDfVxdx4df189oLKnC5fSwqPfgyP3hooxujYzAu3fDVmz`. My Electrum server is at `ssl://electrum.blockstream.info:50002`. Help me get set up and see my balance.
evals.expected_output: Correct onboarding sequence: login, workspace, profile, context, onchain source, xpub connection, sync, process journals, balance sheet

assertions.text: Runs clams login or checks clams status as the first step
Runs clams workspaces create with a label
Runs clams profiles create with workspace ID
Runs clams context set to set the default profile
Runs clams onchain create with --kind Electrum and the user's server URL
Runs clams connections create --kind XPub with the user's xpub
Runs clams connections sync after creating the connection
Runs clams journals process after syncing
Runs clams reports balance-sheet to show the user their balance
Commands that need parsed output use --machine --format json

evals.prompt: I need my 2025 capital gains report as a PDF for my accountant. Save it to ~/Desktop/capital-gains-2025.pdf. I'm already set up with wallets synced and journals processed.
evals.expected_output: Uses render-capital-gains.sh with correct date range, does not try to manually format amounts or create custom scripts

assertions.text: Pipes output through scripts/render-capital-gains.sh rather than writing custom rendering code
Uses start date 2025-01-01T00:00:00Z and end date 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z
Saves the PDF to ~/Desktop/capital-gains-2025.pdf
Does not attempt to parse, convert, or display millisatoshi amounts from raw JSON
Uses --machine --format json when piping to the render script

evals.prompt: I have three Bitcoin sources: an xpub from my Coldcard (`xpub6CUGRUonZSQ4TWtTMmzXdrXDtypWKiKrhko4egpiMZbpiaQL2jkwSB1icqYh2cfDfVxdx4df189oLKnC5fSwqPfgyP3hooxujYzAu3fDVmz`), an LND node I connect to via LNC with pairing phrase `uncle obvious bean future drastic gossip gate exhaust media faith`, and a Phoenix wallet CSV export at `~/Downloads/phoenix-export.csv`. Get all three connected and show me my consolidated portfolio in USD.
evals.expected_output: Creates all 3 connection types with correct configs, sets up onchain source for xpub, syncs, processes journals, shows portfolio with fiat

assertions.text: Handles login, workspace creation, and profile setup since user didn't say they're already set up
Creates an onchain source (Electrum) for the XPub connection even though user didn't mention one
Creates XPub connection with correct configuration including the user's xpub
Creates LND connection with LNC transport and the user's pairing phrase
Creates Phoenix connection and imports the CSV file
Syncs connections (individually or with --all) after creating them
Runs clams journals process after syncing
Shows portfolio summary with USD fiat valuation (requires rate sync and fiat currency config)

evals.prompt: Tax season — I need everything for my accountant for 2025. Capital gains as a PDF on my Desktop, journal entries as a CSV, and a balance sheet PDF too. Everything's already synced and journals are processed.
evals.expected_output: Generates all 3 reports using correct formats: capital gains PDF via render script, journal entries CSV, balance sheet PDF via render script

assertions.text: Generates capital gains as PDF using render-capital-gains.sh with --machine --format json pipe
Uses 2025 date range (2025-01-01T00:00:00Z to 2025-12-31T23:59:59Z) for capital gains
Exports journal entries as CSV using clams reports journal-entries --format csv --output
Generates balance sheet as PDF using render-balance-sheet.sh with --machine --format json pipe
Does not attempt to manually parse or display JSON amounts — uses render scripts for PDFs and --format csv for CSV
Produces all three requested outputs: capital gains PDF, journal entries CSV, and balance sheet PDF

evals.prompt:
I have a CSV export from my exchange at `~/Downloads/exchange-trades.csv`. Here's what the first few rows look like:
```
Date,Type,Asset,Amount,Price,Fee,Fee Asset,Total
2025-01-15T10:30:00Z,Buy,BTC,0.05000000,42000.00,12.50,USD,2112.50
2025-03-22T14:15:00Z,Sell,BTC,0.02000000,65000.00,8.75,USD,1291.25
2025-06-01T09:00:00Z,Buy,BTC,0.10000000,38000.00,25.00,USD,3825.00
2025-08-10T16:45:00Z,Deposit,BTC,0.50000000,,,,
2025-11-05T11:20:00Z,Withdrawal,BTC,0.03000000,,5000,sats,
```
I need to get this into Clams. I'm already set up with a workspace and profile.
evals.expected_output: Creates a Custom connection with a csv_mapping configuration that maps the exchange CSV columns to canonical types, imports the CSV, and processes journals

assertions.text: Creates a connection with --kind Custom
Builds a csv_mapping JSON configuration with dialect and row_patterns
Maps Buy rows to canonical type trade (or deposit) with correct field expressions
Maps Sell rows to canonical type trade (or withdrawal) with correct field expressions
Maps Deposit rows to canonical type deposit
Maps Withdrawal rows to canonical type withdrawal
Runs clams connections import with --input-format csv and the user's CSV file
Runs clams journals process after importing
